mnemonics.title: Kernel-Know-Zero Rule
mnemonics.content: KERNEL: 'Keep' only 'Zero' in mind. A transformation is 1-1 iff kernel contains only the zero vector (ker = {0}). Think: 'K for Keep, Zero in Pocket'.

mnemonics.title: Rank-Nullity Rainbow
mnemonics.content: RANK + NULLITY = DIMENSION of domain. Remember: 'Rank Never Equals Dim' - think of 'RN = Dim' where N stands for Nullity. The domain's dimension is split between rank and nullity.

mnemonics.title: Image Into Codomain
mnemonics.content: IMAGE = 'I'm' in the codomain W. The image always lives inside the codomain. Remember: 'Im' sounds like 'in me' - inside W.

acronyms.expansion: Kernel (Null Space) - set of vectors mapping to zero
acronyms.usage: Used when finding solutions to homogeneous system Ax = 0

acronyms.expansion: Image (Range/Column Space) - set of all output vectors
acronyms.usage: Used when finding if transformation is onto or computing span of outputs
Used to find unknown dimension when one is known

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a funnel where all inputs that end up at zero 'drain' into the zero output. The kernel is the collection of all inputs that get 'crushed' to zero. Draw a transformation as a mapping with vectors flowing - those that collapse to origin form the kernel.

Think of the transformation as a light source casting shadows. The image is the 'shadow' or 'footprint' left on the codomain. The kernel is what's needed to produce that shadow - the 'lost information'.

Imagine a budget: domain dimension is your total budget. Rank is spent on 'useful output' dimension, nullity is 'lost' to kernel. They must balance to equal total dimension. Draw a pie chart showing domain split into two parts.

analogies.analogy: Kernel is like a shredder: documents (vectors) that go in and emerge as blank (zero). The more shredded (larger kernel), the less information preserved in output.
Image is like your WiFi range. Kernel is dead zones where signals don't reach. A transformation is 'onto' if WiFi covers entire house (codomain).
Domain dimension is your salary. Rank is savings (useful/output dimension), nullity is expenses (lost to kernel). They must balance exactly - you can't spend more than you earn!
